headline: Unfinished Business: De Bruyne Is Fit, Lukaku Is Not — and the Golden Generation Gets One Last Shot
subheadline: Belgium's aging stars seek revenge for their 2022 elimination, with De Bruyne declared fit but Lukaku doubtful in a generational clash with Modric's Croatia
openingNarrative:
The ghost of Al Janoub Stadium hangs over this match like a specter that Belgium cannot escape. On December 1, 2022, Belgium — the number-two ranked team in the world — was eliminated from the tournament by a 0-0 draw against Croatia. Romelu Lukaku, Belgium's all-time leading scorer, missed chance after chance in the final minutes, each miss more agonizing than the last. The camera found Kevin De Bruyne's face at the final whistle: fury, disbelief, and the shattering realization that the golden generation's finest opportunity had slipped away.

Now, four years later, Belgium and Croatia meet again. And the narrative has taken a dramatic twist that nobody saw coming. De Bruyne — who was a major fitness doubt heading into the spring, with concerns about his aging body's ability to sustain another tournament campaign — has been declared fit. The relief in Belgian football was palpable. Their March results reflected it: a stunning 5-2 demolition of the United States that showcased Belgium's attacking depth, followed by a 1-1 draw against Mexico that demonstrated tactical flexibility. Youri Tielemans has assumed the captaincy with quiet authority, and the system functions with renewed purpose.

But the injury gods giveth and taketh away. Romelu Lukaku and Leandro Trossard are both doubtful with fitness concerns that cloud Belgium's attacking options. The man whose missed chances defined the 2022 Croatia match may not be available for the rematch. Belgium must decide: is that a crisis or a liberation?

Croatia, by contrast, seem immune to the passage of time. Luka Modric, now 40, continues to defy biological logic. Croatia finished third at the 2022 tournament, reached the 2018 final, and qualified for 2026 with a dominant campaign: 7 wins, 1 draw, 0 losses.

tacticalBreakdown.title: The Chess Match
tacticalBreakdown.narrative:
The 2022 meeting was a tactical stalemate that suited Croatia and devastated Belgium. This time, Belgium cannot afford the same caution — and the fitness doubts around Lukaku and Trossard may force a tactical reinvention that proves to be a blessing in disguise.

The 5-2 dismantling of the USA showed what Belgium look like when De Bruyne is unleashed. Five goals, fluid attacking movement, and a creativity that overwhelmed a decent American defense. Without Lukaku's physical presence as a focal point, Belgium may deploy a false nine or use Openda's pace in behind — a more dynamic approach that could catch Croatia's experienced but aging center-backs off guard.

Tielemans as captain provides midfield stability, and his deep-lying passing complements De Bruyne's more advanced creative role. The 1-1 draw against Mexico showed Belgium can be pragmatic when needed, but the USA result proved the attacking firepower remains devastating when De Bruyne is the conductor.

Croatia's midfield control remains their calling card. Modric's ability to dictate tempo, Kovacic's press-resistance, and Brozovic's defensive screening create a midfield triangle that is arguably the most technically accomplished at the tournament. But at 40, Modric cannot sustain high-intensity pressing for 90 minutes — and Belgium's March performances suggest they will test Croatia's stamina relentlessly.
tacticalBreakdown.keyMatchup: Kevin De Bruyne vs Luka Modric — a generational midfield duel, with De Bruyne declared fit and Modric defying age at 40

dataInsights.title: By The Numbers
dataInsights.eloAnalysis: Belgium (ELO: 1748, rank 8) vs Croatia (ELO: 1720, rank 13) — a narrow 28-point gap that reflects how evenly matched these sides are.
dataInsights.formAnalysis: Belgium's March was emphatic: beat USA 5-2 and drew Mexico 1-1, with De Bruyne declared fit after being a major doubt. Tielemans captaining with authority. Qualified unbeaten: 5 wins, 3 draws, 29 goals scored, De Bruyne contributing 6. Croatia qualified dominantly: 7 wins, 1 draw, 0 losses — 22 points, Kramaric scored 6.
dataInsights.squadValueComparison: Belgium ($771M) vs Croatia ($464M) — Belgium hold a significant financial advantage. But Croatia's Gvardiol ($106M) is the single most valuable player between the two squads.
dataInsights.injuryImpact: The injury picture defines this match. De Bruyne is FIT — the most important three letters in Belgian football. But Lukaku and Trossard are both doubtful, which could force Belgium into a tactical reconfiguration. Lukaku's absence from the 2022 rematch would carry enormous narrative weight — the man whose missed chances defined that night might not get his shot at redemption. Croatia's squad is younger overall, with Gvardiol and Sucic representing the next generation.
dataInsights.bettingPerspective: Belgium's 5-2 win over the USA has boosted confidence, while the Lukaku/Trossard doubts introduce uncertainty. Croatia's consistency makes them reliable tournament performers. This match is likely a coin-toss in the betting markets.
dataInsights.historicalPattern: The 2022 0-0 draw eliminated Belgium — Lukaku's missed chances became one of the tournament's defining images. Croatia went on to finish third. Belgium have never beaten Croatia at a tournament. The psychological scars run deep.

playerSpotlights.name: Kevin De Bruyne
playerSpotlights.narrative: The three most important words in Belgian football right now: De Bruyne fit. After weeks of anxious speculation, Belgium's greatest player has been declared available — and the 5-2 demolition of the USA showed exactly what that means. De Bruyne was imperious: orchestrating attacks, scoring, creating, and playing with the freedom of a man who knows this is his last chance. Against Croatia, in a rematch that has haunted him for four years, De Bruyne will produce the kind of performance that defines a career. This is personal. This is everything.
playerSpotlights.statline: 6 goals in qualifying, declared FIT after being a major doubt — Belgium's tournament depends on him

playerSpotlights.name: Luka Modric
playerSpotlights.narrative: At 40 years old, Modric continues to play football as if time has no dominion over him. The 2018 Ballon d'Or winner's touch, his vision, and his ability to control the tempo of a match remain extraordinary. In 2022, his midfield mastery helped Croatia eliminate Belgium. Now, in what is surely his final tournament, Modric wants one more moment — one more match where he reminds the world that football's most elegant midfielder has lost nothing to the years.
playerSpotlights.statline: 2018 Ballon d'Or winner, Croatia's all-time appearance leader, 40 years old and still magnificent

playerSpotlights.name: Youri Tielemans
playerSpotlights.narrative: The new captain of Belgium has brought a quiet authority that the dressing room needed. Tielemans' deep-lying passing complements De Bruyne's more advanced role, and his leadership — calm, composed, authoritative — has unified a squad that was fractured in 2022. The captaincy is more than an armband; it is a signal that Belgium have moved beyond the ego clashes that undermined their golden generation. Tielemans against Modric in the center of the park is a battle of contrasting styles — the steady metronome against the ageless maestro.
playerSpotlights.statline: Belgium's new captain, providing midfield stability and leadership

playerSpotlights.name: Josko Gvardiol
playerSpotlights.narrative: Manchester City's $106M center-back has evolved from a precocious talent at the 2022 tournament into one of the world's elite defenders. His combination of pace, power, and ball-playing ability makes him a modern defender who can dominate aerial duels and carry the ball 40 yards out of defense. If Lukaku plays, Gvardiol's assignment is clear and familiar. If Belgium play without their traditional target man, Gvardiol must adapt to a more mobile attacking threat.
playerSpotlights.statline: Croatia's most valuable player ($106M), Man City's elite center-back

theVerdict.title: The Verdict
theVerdict.narrative:
The narrative has shifted since 2022. De Bruyne is fit and firing — the 5-2 destruction of the USA proved his creative genius is undimmed. Tielemans' captaincy has brought stability. And the potential absence of Lukaku, while painful in the narrative sense, may actually liberate Belgium tactically. Without the obligation to play through a target man, De Bruyne's creativity can flow through faster, more mobile channels.

Croatia remain formidable — Modric's midfield mastery, Gvardiol's defensive excellence, and their innate tournament know-how make them dangerous opponents. But at 40, Modric cannot sustain the same intensity for 90 minutes, and Belgium's March form suggests they will push Croatia's stamina to its limits.

Expect De Bruyne to produce the match-winning performance that has eluded him at tournaments — perhaps a driving run and shot from distance, perhaps a set-piece delivery that finds an unmarked head. Croatia will equalize through their inevitable midfield quality, but a late Belgian winner — driven by the memory of 2022 and De Bruyne's determination — will settle the score.

This time, Belgium take the three points. The 2022 ghost can finally rest.

alternativeScenarios.reasoning: History repeats in a different key. Another tight, tactical encounter where neither side can establish dominance. A draw that frustrates Belgium and suits Croatia's tournament approach.

Croatia's tournament pedigree proves decisive again. Modric controls the match one final time, and Belgium's golden generation ends with another Croatian defeat. The curse was never about Lukaku's misses — it was about Croatia being better.

closingLine: In 2022, a 0-0 draw ended Belgium's tournament and Lukaku's dignity. Four years later, De Bruyne is fit, Tielemans wears the armband, and Belgium arrive transformed by a 5-2 demolition of the USA. But Lukaku may not be there for his redemption. The rematch demands a different story — and Belgium finally have the pieces to write it.
